summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a legendary Boston Ark show, highlighted by a soaring 30‑minute Dark Star and superb Mountains of the Moon transition.
review: The recording delivers crisp, well‑balanced soundboard audio that lets the band’s 1969 power shine through, despite some tape hiss, early‑set mic feedback and a major cut in The Other One. Musically the performance is top‑tier – Dark Star, Mountains of the Moon, The Eleven and Death Don’t Have No Mercy are repeatedly praised as standout moments, making this one of the era’s most celebrated shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.6★; overwhelmingly positive comments on both performance and mix outweigh a few technical flaws, yielding a high overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑mixed soundboard with excellent stereo imaging; minor tape hiss and occasional mic feedback early in the first set, plus a major splice loss in The Other One and sporadic tape glitches.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Set flows smoothly – a solid opening with 'Sitting on Top of the World' leads into a magical Mountains‑of‑the‑Moon → Dark Star sequence, followed by tight second‑set jams; transitions are praised and the overall setlist is considered exemplary for 1969.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for raw energy, electrifying guitar tone and soaring vocals, especially on Dark Star and Death Don't Have No Mercy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as a 'monster' on The Other One and providing strong chord work that underpins the jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s organ is highlighted for adding depth; keyboards noted as prominent and well‑balanced in the mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy & Mickey’s drumming receives high marks for tight stereo placement, interplay and driving the extended improvisations.
